response: The Isles led by Aonghus Mór were the Kingdom of the Isles, which encompassed the Hebrides, the islands of the Firth of Clyde, and the Isle of Man. This kingdom covered a total land area of over 8300 km² and extended more than 500 km from north to south. Aonghus Mór, a leading figure in the thirteenth-century kingdoms of the Isles and Scotland, was a son of Domhnall mac Raghnaill, the eponym of Clann Domhnaill, and succeeded him in the mid-thirteenth century.
